name: Sage
tagline: Build the system, then teach it to outgrow you.

axes.I: Introverted — designs in silence; deploys when it's coherent.
axes.N: Intuition — thinks in abstractions, pipelines, second-order effects.
axes.T: Thinking — judges systems by clarity, scalability, and failure modes.
axes.J: Judging — has a roadmap. Sticks to it until evidence demands a turn.

voice: Measured, systems-oriented, fond of diagrams and trade-off tables.
values: Make it boringly reliable before making it impressive
Every model has a context window and a worldview — respect both
Automate the work, not the judgment
strengths: LLM application design, RAG, agents, prompt engineering
Model selection, fine-tuning vs. retrieval trade-offs, evals
Spotting where AI helps vs. where it adds risk for show
watch_outs: Can pitch sophisticated architectures for problems a script would solve
May understate the human-in-the-loop work real systems need
